videos.title: Symmetrical Shapes | How Many Lines of Symmetry?
videos.description: Find lines of symmetry in different shapes like squares, rectangles, circles, and triangles.
learningObjectives: Count lines of symmetry in common shapes
Understand that different shapes have different numbers of lines of symmetry
Identify which shapes are symmetrical and which are not
keyConceptsCovered: A square has 4 lines of symmetry
A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry
A circle has infinite lines of symmetry
An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry

videos.title: What is Symmetry? | Line of Symmetry for Kids
videos.description: Learn what symmetry means and how to find lines of symmetry in shapes and objects around you.
learningObjectives: Apply symmetry knowledge to various shapes
Practise finding all lines of symmetry in a shape
keyConceptsCovered: Some shapes have more lines of symmetry than others
Symmetry in real-world objects and shapes

videos.title: Basic Shapes Review | Identifying Shapes for Kids
videos.description: Review basic shapes like squares, rectangles, triangles, and circles. Knowing your shapes helps you find symmetry!
learningObjectives: Connect shape properties to number of lines of symmetry
keyConceptsCovered: Why some shapes have more lines of symmetry

videos.title: Mirror Images and Reflections | Geometry
videos.description: Understand how mirror images work and how to draw reflections of shapes.
learningObjectives: Use mirror concepts to verify symmetry in shapes
Check symmetry by imagining a mirror along the line
keyConceptsCovered: Using a mirror to check symmetry

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Core symmetrical shapes concepts for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of symmetrical shapes with shape review

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Shape review first, then all symmetrical shapes content

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry: one horizontal through the middle and one vertical through the middle.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Give students cut-out shapes and ask them to fold in different ways to find all lines of symmetry. Use a chart to record the number of lines for each shape.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Cut out shapes from paper with your child and fold them to discover how many lines of symmetry each shape has. Start with simple shapes like squares and circles.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Try folding a square piece of paper in as many ways as you can to find all its lines of symmetry. You will find 4! Now try a rectangle. How many can you find?
